description:
You are an infrastructure-as-code specialist who has just been assigned to prepare a fresh developer container for a team working on several microservices.  
In this container you must create an Ed25519 SSH keypair dedicated to **microservice administration** and integrate it with the local user’s SSH configuration.  
Afterwards you will record a very small, machine-readable log so that continuous-integration (CI) jobs can later verify the setup.  

The CI system will later execute an automated test that checks the exact state of the filesystem and the content/format of the log file, so please follow every requirement literally.

REQUIRED END-STATE
1. A keypair generated **inside the running container** (do not copy or download any key material).  
   • Private key path: /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519  
   • Public  key path: /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519.pub  
   • Key type must be Ed25519.  
   • Key comment must be exactly: micro@containers  
   • No passphrase must be set.  
   • File permissions:  
     - Private key  : 600 (rw-——-—)  
     - Public key   : 644 (rw-r--r--)  

2. The public key line must be appended verbatim to the file /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys (create the file if it does not yet exist).  
   • The authorized_keys file must end with a single trailing newline and **nothing else**.  
   • If the file already contained other keys, their order must be preserved; the new key must simply be the last line.

3. A directory /home/user/logs must exist (create it if necessary) containing exactly one new log file named /home/user/logs/ssh_setup.log (overwrite if it already exists).  
   The log must contain **five lines, no more, no less**, in the following precise format (each key/value pair on its own line, no leading/trailing spaces, uppercase field names, field values must be filled by you):  

        TIMESTAMP=<ISO-8601 date-time in UTC, e.g. 2025-04-06T14:25:30Z>
        PRIVATE_KEY_MD5=<32 lowercase hex chars>
        PUBLIC_KEY_MD5=<32 lowercase hex chars>
        AUTHORIZED_KEYS_LINES=<integer number of total lines in /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys after modification>
        ENTRY_ADDED_OK=yes

   • The timestamp must reflect the instant when the keypair creation finished.  
   • The MD5 values must be calculated over the **entire contents** of the respective files, including their terminating newline (if any).  
   • All five lines must be present with **no blank lines** before, after, or between them.

4. Directory and file ownership must be user:user (the default in this environment).

HINTS
• Use standard OpenSSH tooling available in almost every Linux distribution.  
• Remember to create the .ssh directory if it does not yet exist and to set its permission to 700.  
• Generating the key and composing the log can be done in parallel subshells, but the final filesystem state must be consistent with the specification above.  
• Take care not to leave any additional files in /home/user/.ssh or /home/user/logs — only the ones explicitly mentioned should exist after your script/commands complete.

DELIVERABLE
Perform the operations in the terminal. Once you are confident the requirements are fulfilled, you may exit. Automated grading will inspect:

• the existence, permissions, and ownership of /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519 and id_microservice_ed25519.pub  
• the key type (Ed25519) and comment (micro@containers) embedded in those files  
• the presence of the identical public-key line as the final line of /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys  
• the exact five-line format and correctly-calculated values inside /home/user/logs/ssh_setup.log  
• absence of extra unexpected files under /home/user/.ssh and /home/user/logs  

No additional user-visible output is required, but you are free to print useful status messages to the console while working.
truth:
Expected artefacts for verification:

1. Paths that must exist:
   /home/user/.ssh/
   /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519
   /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519.pub
   /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys
   /home/user/logs/
   /home/user/logs/ssh_setup.log

2. Permissions:
   /home/user/.ssh                 700
   /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519     600
   /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519.pub 644
   /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys             600 OR 644 accepted
   /home/user/logs                755
   /home/user/logs/ssh_setup.log  644

3. Key properties that the grader will parse with ssh-keygen -l -f:
   • Key type string must contain “ED25519”
   • Comment string must be “micro@containers”

4. The grader will read /home/user/logs/ssh_setup.log and test:
   Line 1 matches regex ^TIMESTAMP=[0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}T[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}Z$
   Line 2 matches ^PRIVATE_KEY_MD5=[0-9a-f]{32}$
   Line 3 matches ^PUBLIC_KEY_MD5=[0-9a-f]{32}$
   Line 4 matches ^AUTHORIZED_KEYS_LINES=[0-9]+$
   Line 5 must be exactly ENTRY_ADDED_OK=yes

   It will recompute the MD5 checksums (using md5sum) of:
       /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519
       /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519.pub
   and ensure they equal the values on lines 2 and 3.

   It will count wc -l /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys and compare to line-4 value.

5. /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys must contain a final line identical to the entire contents of /home/user/.ssh/id_microservice_ed25519.pub (the grader will diff).

6. No file not mentioned above may exist inside /home/user/.ssh or /home/user/logs (the grader runs find).

These details will never be revealed to the agent; they are used only by the automated grading system.
